mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through this mechanical valuation channel.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Dec 2006 reflects recent Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate earnings growth expectations. When PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month, equity investors revise earnings forecasts upward, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,759 thousand as of Dec 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) directly reflect forward-looking builder confidence and consumer demand for residential investment. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1,500 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations that cascade into reduced corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,500 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,572 thousand as of Nov 2006, near cyclical peak
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of 60-day windows following HOUST drops below 1,500 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. When INDPRO decelerates below its 3-month moving average by more than 0.5 percentage points, it signals supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth, producing negative 5-day forward returns as investors reprrice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls 0.5pp below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.0951 as of Dec 2006, near trend
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer and residential demand momentum. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously month-over-month, it signals broad-based demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline AND HOUST month-over-month decline in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 620,214 million as of Nov 2006, HOUST at 1,572 thousand
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of co-declining instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly capture labor market tightness. When UNRATE rises above 6.0% while PAYEMS growth slows below 100,000 persons month-over-month, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate earnings, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.9% as of Dec 2006, near cyclical lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.0% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals monetary policy constraint that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. This regime produces negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 270.981 as of Dec 2006, FEDFUNDS at 5.25%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate compared to the prior quarter, it signals demand weakness that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns in the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,086.375 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2006
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
